vulnerability.title: Prevent Unrestricted Mail Relaying
vulnerability.description: Modify the /etc/postfix/main.cf file to restrict client connections to the local network with the following command: $ sudo postconf -e 'smtpd_client_restrictions = permit_mynetworks,reject'
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && rpm --quiet -q postfix; then if ! grep -q ^smtpd_client_restrictions /etc/postfix/main.cf; then echo "smtpd_client_restrictions = permit_mynetworks,reject" >> /etc/postfix/main.cf else sed -i "s/^smtpd_client_restrictions.*/smtpd_client_restrictions = permit_mynetworks,reject/g" /etc/postfix/main.cf fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves a straightforward configuration change to Postfix’s main.cf, which is a standard hardening step that does not risk SSH or console lockout and is unlikely to break critical services.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential temporary mail service disruption if the configuration is incorrect

execution_details.stderr: ssh: connect to host 192.168.1.53 port 22: Permission denied
ssh: connect to host 192.168.1.53 port 22: Permission denied

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for postfix_prevent_unrestricted_relay (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_postfix_prevent_unrestricted_relay). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message: I was unable to obtain `/etc/postfix/main.cf` because the SSH connection was denied, preventing me from reading or modifying the file safely.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will effectively add or replace the required smtpd_client_restrictions line and thus prevents unrestricted relaying. However, it does not reload Postfix to apply the change and could override other important restrictions. It follows good practice by checking RPM packages, but could be improved for completeness.
concerns: Does not trigger Postfix reload, so configuration changes may not take effect.
May overwrite other existing client restrictions that include additional parameters, such as permit_sasl_authenticated or reject_unauth_destination.
Command quoting could cause errors if the system’s shell interprets the single quotes incorrectly.
The script only checks for Postfix presence on RPM based systems; other distributions will skip remediation without notification.
suggested_improvements:
Add a postfix reload or restart after modifying main.cf.
Use postconf -e to change the setting instead of manual sed/echo.
Include reject_unauth_destination to ensure no open relay.
Test the pattern replacement to preserve whitespace and comments.
Add a conditional to inform the user if the setting was already correctly configured.
